question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: The symptom where migrated users can sign into cloud services but on-prem applications report “user not found / not recognized” matches a documented UPN-suffix / provisioning error that required reversing on-prem userPrincipalName changes and running a full Azure AD Connect sync to restore on‑prem authentication (TI-1700001). This was not a simple helpdesk fix in the record but required identity‑team changes to AAD Connect/provisioning

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: The inconsistent group memberships (on‑prem shows new groups while cloud still shows old groups) matches a case where the new group objects were placed in an OU outside the AAD Connect sync scope; the fix was to update the AAD Connect OU filter and perform a full sync (TI-1700002) — an infrastructure change handled by engineers
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 54:
 06.01.25.
Thanks!

28.01.25 14:27 Ticket closed | TI-1700002
Closure description:
Root cause was that the new group structure created for the second migration wave was placed in an OU outside the Azure AD Connect organizational unit filter scope. The old groups remained synced because they were in the original sync scope. As a result, the cloud directory continued to evaluate users' membership against the (synchronized) old groups, while on-prem evaluated against the (renamed/recreated) new groups.
Resolution: Updated the Azure AD Connect OU filter to include the new "Migration_Phase2" OU containing the new group objects. Performed a full synchronization. Validated that affected users now have correct group memberships in both M365 and on-prem systems.

Notes:
16.01.25 11:13 Admin, CRM
Ticket opened.
17.01.25 09:55 Employee 30
Initial assessment: customer is the same KF70 hybrid migration as TI-1700001. Different symptom, possibly related root cause area (AAD Connect configuration during migration).
20.01.25 14:00 Employee 30
Confirmed: affected users have correct on-prem AD group membership but the cloud-side membership shows the old groups. This indicates the new groups are not being synced to Entra ID.
21.01.25 10:45 Employee 30
Reviewed AAD Connect configuration. The group filter (organizational unit-based) was set up for the original directory layout. The new groups for the second migration wave were placed in a new OU "Migration_Phase2" which was not included in the AAD Connect sync scope.
24.01.25 11:20 Employee 30
Updated OU filter to include Migration_Phase2. Ran initial delta sync to confirm new group objects appear in Entra ID, then ran full sync to ensure all member relationships propagate correctly.
27.01.25 09:30 Employee 30
Customer validated that affected users now show correct group memberships in M365 and SharePoint permissions are evaluating correctly.
28.01.25 14:27 Microsoft CRM Portals
Ticket closed.

answer: The built-in mobile clients failing while Outlook mobile worked corresponds to a tenant-level Modern Authentication enforcement issue that required switching clients to modern auth and re-enrolling devices (TI-1700003) — again handled by engineers/endpoint team and not a pure first-line password reset

27.01.25 10:18 Ticket closed | TI-1700003
Closure description:

Built-in iOS Mail and Android Gmail clients on older device versions still attempted Basic Auth via the EAS protocol, which is now blocked. Compounding factor: For users from the second migration wave, the built-in mail clients had cached credentials from the on-prem-only configuration before migration. After migration, the credentials needed to be removed and the account re-added using a method that supports modern authentication.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Scheduled tasks run by service accounts failing due to authentication aligns with a case where service accounts were unintentionally targeted by a tenant Conditional Access policy requiring MFA; resolution involved creating an exclusion group and adjusting CA/AAD Connect handling (TI-1700004). That required changes to CA configuration and AAD Connect scope by the identity/infra team
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 50:
 you mention Conditional Access: as part of the migration baseline a CA policy was enabled requiring MFA for the "KF70-AllUsers-Hybrid" group. Could that be the issue?

31.01.25 16:34 Ticket closed | TI-1700004
Closure description:
Root cause was twofold. First, the service accounts were placed in the "KF70-AllUsers-Hybrid" group, which is the group targeted by the new tenant-baseline Conditional Access policy "KF70-Require-MFA". The Conditional Access evaluation was therefore being applied to non-interactive logons for service accounts, breaking scheduled-task authentication where MFA cannot be satisfied. Second, the service accounts had been moved to the new OU which is filtered IN by Azure AD Connect, leading to the accounts being synchronized to Entra ID and then having a license assigned (small but non-zero impact on the on-prem authentication path because of mfaPreferred attribute conflicts).
Resolution: Created a sub-group "KF70-ServiceAccounts-NoMFA" containing the service accounts and added it to the Conditional Access policy as an explicit exclusion. Re-evaluated AAD Connect filter to keep the on-prem service-account authentication path clean. Validated that scheduled tasks are now running successfully overnight.
Documentation note: For future migrations, service accounts must be explicitly excluded from baseline CA policies via a dedicated exclusion group, and their AAD Connect sync status should be reviewed to avoid unintended cloud-side license assignment. We have added this to the migration runbook.

Notes:
21.01.25 08:02 Admin, CRM
Ticket opened.
21.01.25 08:20 Admin, CRM
Priority elevated due to operational impact.
24.01.25 10:00 Employee 30
Initial diagnosis: scheduled tasks fail with logon-type denied. Customer confirms recent migration changed service-account group membership. Strong suspicion the new tenant-baseline Conditional Access policy is being evaluated against non-interactive service-account logons.
27.01.25 14:30 Employee 30
Confirmed via Azure AD sign-in logs: failed sign-in attempts for the service accounts are tagged "Failed - Conditional Access requires MFA". The CA policy targets the All-Users-Hybrid group which includes the service accounts.
28.01.25 11:00 Employee 30
Created KF70-ServiceAccounts-NoMFA exclusion group. Added service accounts. Updated CA policy to exclude this group. Sign-in logs now show successful authentication.
30.01.25 09:30 Employee 30
Customer confirms scheduled tasks ran successfully overnight without errors. Asked them to monitor for one more cycle before closure.
31.01.25 16:34 Microsoft CRM Portals
Ticket closed. Migration runbook updated with service-account handling guidance.
